subject: Update oparent version to 1.1.0
commit_message:
Update oparent version to 1.1.0

Directive from ONAP project that all repos should be
updated. Change vierified with a build with .m2 /org/onap
cleared.

Changed dependency org.onap.msb.java-sdki.msb-java-sdk from
version 1.1.0-SNAPSHOT to version 1.1.0

Change-Id: I46abc49019cb81b726e3f71ca8fe1beed621a426
Issue-ID: POLICY-693
Signed-off-by: liamfallon <liam.fallon@ericsson.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-applications-master-verify-java/527/

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-applications-master-verify-java/527/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-drools-applications-master-verify-java/527

Patch Set 1:

I understood from some communication from Gary (I am questioning if I may have been misunderstood) that oparent  version does check for SNAPSHOT dependencies outside this repo.   I thought they were ones in this repo that refer to other repos SNAPSHOT versions, if I recall correctly MSB one, and probably others, so I was surprised it built with this oparent version.  At some point, I think we need a work item to convert SNAPSHOT references to released ones, that can be another activity.   In any case, not sure how this built in any case, I may have misunderstood Gary on the check on SNAPSHOT dependencies ??

Patch Set 1:

When this JIRA was assigned to me I changed the oparent version on a clean clone and tried the build, which worked. I'm afraid I didn't do any deep analysis as I thought I'd try it first and see what happened.

The only snapshot reference in this repo is the one below.
/controlloop/common/msb/pom.xml
    <dependency>
      <groupId>org.onap.msb.java-sdk</groupId>
      <artifactId>msb-java-sdk</artifactId>
      <version>1.1.0-SNAPSHOT</version>
    </dependency>

I see that there is a 1.1.0 version of that dependency in the ONAP staging nexus now so we cold change that one. I just changed it on my local copy and ran a build which worked. We should change that dependency.
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

Jorge, we do not need a work item to be created. This version of oparent will fail in the release jobs if there is a SNAPSHOT version. But of course we will be follow the guidelines as directed by ONAP versioning and release process. I sent you this previously.

Patch Set 2:

Let me clarify, the previous version of oparent did NOT check. This one does. Its the LF Jenkins Jobs that do not allow staging jobs to succeed if any SNAPSHOTS are being used. Both are checkpoints.
